message:
feat(mobile): improve sequence view zoom defaults for better readability

**Problem:**
The sequence grid was using zoom levels optimized for desktop that were
too small on mobile phones, making nucleotides hard to read. Users had
to manually zoom in every time they opened the app.

**Solution:**
Increase default zoom levels on mobile to match user expectations:

packages/web/src/hooks/useSequenceGrid.ts:
- Small phones (<480px): 1.1 → 1.4 zoom (27% larger)
- Larger phones (<768px): 1.05 → 1.2 zoom (14% larger)
- Tablets/desktop: unchanged at 1.0

The new values were chosen to:
1. Make individual nucleotides clearly readable without squinting
2. Show enough context to understand sequence structure
3. Match the renderer's own mobile-aware defaults for consistency

packages/web/src/components/SequenceView.tsx:
- Changed initialZoomScale fallback from hardcoded density-based value
  to `undefined`, letting the renderer pick mobile-aware defaults
- Previously: `storeZoomScale ?? (densityMode === 'compact' ? 0.85 : 1.0)`
- Now: `storeZoomScale ?? undefined`
- This ensures first-run users get appropriate zoom for their device

packages/web/src/components/mobile/BottomSheet.tsx:
- Fixed TypeScript error with CSS custom property type casting
- Changed `as React.CSSProperties` to `as unknown as React.CSSProperties`
- Required because custom properties (--bottom-sheet-*) aren't in the
  CSSProperties type definition

These changes work together with the earlier mobile UX fixes (viewport
zoom prevention, horizontal scroll locking) to provide a polished
mobile experience out of the box.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
